title: Thelypteris albidipilosa

Thelypteris albidipilosa is native to Central and South America, specifically in countries such as Mexico, Costa Rica, and Panama. This species is part of the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. Thelypteris albidipilosa is also sometimes classified as a member of the family Pteridaceae due to ongoing taxonomic debates.

intro: Thelypteris albidipilosa, also known as the silver shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris albidipilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
